mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rate (UNRATE > 7%) indicates substantial labor market slack, which suppresses wage growth and inflation expectations, anchoring long-term Treasury yields at low levels. With no imminent tightening, yields are expected to remain flat to lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward yield changes are positive in >40% of windows while UNRATE > 7%, or the 60-day moving average of yields increases by more than 25 bps.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate is at 0.09%, effectively at the zero lower bound. Should the Fed raise rates to 0.25% or higher, it would signal the beginning of a tightening cycle, steepening the yield curve and pushing 10-year yields higher as term premiums increase.
failure_condition: After fed funds rises above 0.25%, 10-year yields decline in more than 50% of subsequent 20-day windows, or yield change is never >5 bps within 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) rises above 100 and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 7%, it signals a synchronized recovery in production and employment, boosting growth expectations and driving 10-year yields higher.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, 10-year yields do not increase by more than 10 bps on average over the next 20 trading days, or the rank correlation between the joint signal and yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound environment (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), an unexpected rise in retail sales (RSAFS) signals stronger consumer demand, which lifts growth expectations and pushes yields higher. In contrast, in a normal rate environment (FEDFUNDS > 2%), the same rise would tighten financial conditions and lower yields.
failure_condition: If, while FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, positive RSAFS changes are associated with yield declines in more than 60% of occurrences, or the directional accuracy is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,000,000 units signal strong residential investment, which propels GDP growth and raises inflation expectations, causing 10-year yields to rise.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1000, 10-year yields decline in more than 40% of subsequent 20-day windows, or the average yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 100 (index) indicates that output has recovered to pre-recession levels, which alleviates supply constraints and reduces upward pressure on prices, leading to lower 10-year yields as inflation expectations moderate.
failure_condition: After INDPRO crosses above 100, 10-year yields increase in more than 60% of subsequent 20-day windows, or the average yield change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS above 136,000) and retail sales (RSAFS above 430,000) provides a composite signal of strong consumer-led economic momentum, which drives up growth expectations and 10-year yields.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the 5-day yield change is negative in more than 50% of cases, or the average 5-day change is less than 2 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP reading above 16,000 billion chained dollars (GDPC1) signals that the economy has expanded beyond its pre-recession peak, driving robust growth expectations that push 10-year yields higher.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds 16000, 10-year yields decline in more than 50% of subsequent 20-day windows, or the average yield change is negative.
